quote_of_day.text: I wonder if these babus and netas even have conscience, I get them, I understand getting power in lundia is one time opportunity to secure future of your family for generations to come, but still, how much do you need? why go to such lengths? how do they even sleep at night?
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred and insults moved over the board today, bringing with it a torrent of abusive language.

Anon launched a vicious attack on an IT executive, calling them a 'simp' and a 'baniya' in a lengthy tirade.

A cold front of despair and hopelessness swept through the discussion thread on Lundia, with one anon declaring that the country has no future and suggesting that poor people should simply end their lives.

This bleak outlook seemed to hang in the air, with no clear respite in sight. Meanwhile, a scattered drizzle of pollution complaints fell across the board, with one anon posting a photo of polluted Kasol in Himachal Pradesh.

A brief but intense downpour of slurs and insults accompanied the usual discussions, including a definition of 'pajeet' that sparked a brief flurry of responses.

A weak high-pressure system of indecision seemed to stall over the thread about Modi, with one post drawing a blank response.

articles.title: Anon Calls IT Executive a 'Simp' and 'Baniya' in Online Tirade
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion with a one-word post: 'BROOTAL'. One anon responded with a lengthy critique of an IT executive, referring to him as 'simpjeet' and 'baniya', claiming he tried to create a 'westernized aestheticmaxxed romantic video' but failed.

This anon also mentioned that the executive got 'cvked' and died in a '400 feet deep naali'. Another anon chimed in, saying General, OBC, and other categories don't guarantee social status, calling OBC 'just means backward dumb chamar'.

Anon also discussed an Instagram user, Siya Rawal, with a post that seemed to mock her, calling her 'gobar' and referencing her possible connections to high-status individuals. One respondent noted Rawal's LinkedIn profile and claimed she wasn't as refined as she seemed.

The conversation took a turn as anons started comparing social and caste dynamics. One claimed baniyas aren't even considered 'vasihyas', while others like Jats, Gurjars, and Yadavs are seen as 'honourary kshtriyas'.

A different anon argued that during the Sultanate period, baniyas were more powerful, funding military expeditions while Rajputs had to submit.

Throughout the discussion, various anons used derogatory terms to refer to individuals and groups, making claims about their social status, financial power, and physical appearance. The conversation remained fixated on these perceived social hierarchies and the relationships between different groups.

articles.title: Lundia's Corruption and Despair
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion by stating that even the British did not loot Lundia as much as its current leaders have, and that if you're poor in Lundia, you should just kill yourself.

Another anon wondered if Lundia's bureaucrats and politicians have any conscience, understanding that getting power in Lundia is an opportunity to secure their family's future, but questioning how much they need and how they sleep at night.

A user named Punjabi suggested that it would be better if Khalistan were formed, and that a pan-India Hindu identity is a utopian dream that will never happen, as everyone wants to eat one another.

Anon described reading about Lundia's corruption as demoralizing, especially for common people who suffer to afford a basic lifestyle, and imagined how depressing it must be for rural poor people living in jhopdas and urban slums.

Another anon mentioned that a similar incident had occurred in Madhya Pradesh a few weeks ago, calling it the most corrupt state in Lundia, and lamenting that there was a time when such corruption was at least considered an issue.

In response to a comment about the poor not being able to read, an anon suggested that most of them can't read English, and that the poor don't cry because they know there's no hope.

The discussion took a personal turn when an anon made a derogatory comment about Punjabi Hindus. The thread had various brief responses, including 'aye aye captain' and 'nah not a big deal', which seemed to be reactions to preceding comments.

articles.title: Pollution in Himachal Pradesh
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying "This is kasol, HP". One anon replied, expressing frustration with pollution in Himachal Pradesh, citing their personal experience of witnessing locals littering in Kullu and Manali.

They described finding piles of cow and dog excrement everywhere. Another anon asked if there were any "legitimately 'clean'" Indian states, citing their own experiences of finding similar levels of pollution in Uttar Pradesh, Mumbai, Delhi, and Bangalore.

They described driving through the country as feeling like navigating an "apocalyptic wasteland". A respondent claimed to have visited every district in Himachal Pradesh and found them all equally polluted, except for a couple of upscale neighborhoods in Shimla.

They also mentioned similar conditions in Jammu and on the highway to Leh. One anon suggested that the problem wasn't geographical but demographic, stating that removing "pajeets" would make the land pristine.

They claimed that placing "pajeets" anywhere would turn that place into a "shithole". The conversation also touched on the topic of tourists contributing to pollution, with one anon blaming "Bimaru tourists" for dirtying up parts of Himachal Pradesh.

The discussion included various personal anecdotes and insults, with one participant claiming to be a "research assistant" working 40 hours a week in a postdoctoral research position in agriculture.

The thread also included several exchanges of hostility and personal attacks, with one anon boasting about their testicles being "whiter than the whites of your eyes" and another requesting a picture of someone's testicles.

articles.title: Anon defines 'pajeet' on Indiachan /b/
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking what the term 'pajeet' means. The original poster associated the term with people from the south Asian subcontinent, particularly those they claimed to dislike, encompassing various religions, castes, and backgrounds.

One respondent called the OP a 'pajeet' themselves, suggesting that the term refers to people perceived as uncivilized or lower-class. Another anon provided a link to an Urban Dictionary definition of 'pajeet'.

The discussion quickly devolved into insults and personal attacks, with multiple anons using the term 'pajeet' as an insult, and others referencing 'bimarus' and 'abbos', terms often used to describe people from certain regions or castes in India.

articles.title: Modi slur draws blank response
articles.body: Anon claimed Prime Minister Modi is a 'Ball worshipping Jew Not hindu' and referred to his followers as 'Goyims'. One respondent simply posted '>>22584', which appeared to be a reference to an unrelated post.
